               the Division of Special Publications. The data           Annual averages                                                 Jan.
               are collected by the Bureau of the Census
               (Department of Commerce), State Employment               Revised seasonally adjusted series                              Feb.
               Security Agencies, and State Departments of
               Labor in cooperation with the Bureau of Labor            Quarterly averages: Seasonally adjusted
               Statistics. A brief description of the                    data, persons not in labor force, persons
               cooperative statistical programs of the BLS               of Hispanic origin, Vietnam-Era veterans
               with these agencies is presented in the Ex-               and nonveterans, poverty-nonpoverty area                 Jan., Apr.,
               planatory Notes. The State agencies are listed            data, family relationship data.                           July, Oct.
               on the inside back cover.

                The Secretary of Labor has determined that
                the publication of this periodical is necessary
                in the transaction of the public business re-
                quired by law of this Department. Use of funds
                for printing this periodical has been approved
                by the Director of the Office of Management
                and Budget through February 28, 1981. Con-
                trolled circulation postage paid at Riverdale,
                Md.

                Unless identified as copyrighted, articles and
                tabulations in this publication are in the public
                domain and may be reproduced without per-
                mission of the Federal Government. Please
                credit Employment and Earnings, Bureau of
                Labor Statistics.

                                                                            February 1979. The March 1979 figures were left out of the table; the columns for April, May,

                                                                       Explanatory Notes

                                  These explanatory notes provide information on the concepts, methodology, and scope of Household Data
                                  (A tables), Establishment Data (B, C, and D tables), and State and Area Unemployment Data (E table) pub-
                                  lished in Employment and Earnings.



                                                                               Introduction

                   The statistics in this periodical are compiled from two major            Employment
                sources: (1) Household interviews, and (2) reports from employers.
